Class TreeHitTestData

Contains information about which element of an ITree is located at a specific position.

Inheritance
object
TreeHitTestData
Inherited Members
object.ToString()
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
Namespace: PhiRobotics.UI
Assembly: PhiRobotics.UI.dll
Syntax
public sealed class TreeHitTestData
Remarks

Instances of this class are returned by HitTest(Point) and represent the result of a hit test operation.

A hit test identifies:

  • The ITreeNode at the specified position, if a node exists.
  • The ITreeHitTestLocation describing the exact region of the tree control (for example, node text, node image, expand button, indentation area, or free space).

If the pointer is positioned over an empty region of the tree, the Node property returns null, while the Location property still describes the region that was hit.

Examples

The following example demonstrates how to use HitTest(Point) to determine which part of the tree is located under the mouse pointer.

TreeHitTestData hit = tree.HitTest(mousePoint);

if (hit.Location.IsText)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Node text clicked: {hit.Node.Text}");
}

if (hit.Location.IsExpandButton)
{
    Console.WriteLine("Expand/collapse button clicked.");
}

if (hit.Location.IsFreeArea)
{
    Console.WriteLine("Clicked on empty space.");
}

Constructors

TreeHitTestData(ITreeHitTestLocation, ITreeNode)

Initializes a new instance of the TreeHitTestData class with the specified hit test location and node.

Declaration
public TreeHitTestData(ITreeHitTestLocation location, ITreeNode node)
Parameters
Type Name Description
ITreeHitTestLocation location

The logical region of the tree that was hit.
ITreeNode node

The node associated with the hit test location, or null if the hit occurred in a free area of the tree.

Properties

Location

Gets the logical region of the tree that was hit.

Declaration
public ITreeHitTestLocation Location { get; }
Property Value
Type Description
ITreeHitTestLocation
Remarks

Identifies the exact part of the tree control where the hit occurred, such as node text, image, expand button, indentation area, or free space.

Node

Gets the node associated with the hit test result.

Declaration
public ITreeNode Node { get; }
Property Value
Type Description
ITreeNode
Remarks

Returns the ITreeNode located at the tested position. If the hit test occurred outside any node, this property returns null.
